title: Linderniella andongensis
taxonomy: Linderniella andongensis is classified within the family Linderniaceae, which is a family of flowering plants in the order Lamiales. This species is further classified as a member of the genus Linderniella, which is a small genus of plants. Linderniella andongensis is native to tropical Africa, specifically in the regions of Angola and Mozambique. The species is known to thrive in humid and shaded environments, often growing near rivers and streams. As a member of the family Linderniaceae, Linderniella andongensis is closely related to other species within the family, such as Lindernia and Torenia.

intro: Linderniella andongensis is a medicinal plant species belonging to the Linderniaceae family, native to tropical Africa. It has been traditionally used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, thanks to its reported health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Linderniella andongensis include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Linderniella andongensis is often used to prepare infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are commonly consumed to alleviate symptoms of fever and rheumatism.
